evidence: lib/ is the clear logic layer (12 files, avg fan-in 4.2) and app/api/ is the HTTP boundary — dependency direction is consistently one-way: routes import lib, lib does not import routes.
lib/db.ts (9 importers) and lib/validate.ts (9 importers) are hub modules; any schema change in Prisma or any ValidationError shape change propagates to the majority of the codebase.

lib/search.ts re-exports types from lib/restaurant-grouping.ts (`export type { RestaurantLocation, RestaurantGroup }`) while also wrapping _groupRestaurantsByName with a thin adapter. This is a narrow but real boundary blur: callers importing types from lib/search.ts are actually depending on lib/restaurant-grouping.ts without knowing it.

app/api/scrape/route.ts acts as both HTTP handler and pipeline orchestrator (fetchWithFallback, enrichAndUpsert, upsertRestaurant are all defined inside or alongside the route file). This concentrates orchestration logic in the HTTP layer rather than in lib/, which is a dependency-direction violation consistent with the pre-existing open issue.

lib/fingerprint.ts duplicates IP extraction logic: it reads x-forwarded-for and x-real-ip inline. lib/geo.ts exports extractIp() which does the same. Both modules exist in the same layer and perform identical header parsing. app/api routes that need fingerprinting (vote, restaurant-vote) use lib/fingerprint.ts; routes that need IP for geo (search, trending, geo) use lib/geo.ts extractIp(). This cross-module duplication indicates a missing shared boundary.

components/LoadMoreButton.tsx imports groupRestaurantsByName from lib/restaurant-grouping.ts directly (correct), while lib/search.ts also imports and wraps it (adding an adapter function groupRestaurantsByName). The wrapper in lib/search.ts is only used by app/search/page.tsx, making it a thin shim that blurs the boundary.
No cycles detected in the import graph — the dependency graph is acyclic.
lib/rules.ts is imported by app/api/vote/route.ts and app/api/restaurant-vote/route.ts. The rules module itself imports lib/db.ts. The chain (route → rules → db) is properly directional.

lib/state-machine.ts is used only within app/api/scrape/route.ts. Its primary consumer is in the HTTP layer, which is acceptable since the state machine models the scrape pipeline. However, the advance() callback defined in the route captures local mutable state — a form of cross-module coordination through a closure over mutable state.
package_size_census: lib/ dominates at 1730 LOC across 12 files; components/ is 930 LOC; the codebase is small enough (~34 files, 3901 LOC) that this is expected, not a red flag.

Top-level directory layout (lib/, components/, app/api/, app/search/, app/restaurant/) maps cleanly to domain roles: pure logic, UI primitives, HTTP boundary, and page-level routing. A new engineer can orient quickly.
lib/ files are coherently named by domain concern: geo.ts, reddit.ts, hours.ts, rules.ts, state-machine.ts, restaurant-grouping.ts. Each file has a focused stated responsibility.

OPEN ISSUE CONFIRMED: STATUS_LABELS/STATUS_BADGE/STATUS_INFO maps are defined independently in RestaurantCard.tsx, TrendingSection.tsx, and app/restaurant/[id]/page.tsx. OPEN ISSUE CONFIRMED: app/api/vote/route.ts and app/api/restaurant-vote/route.ts both call tryTransitionToVerified() after the transaction commits. If tryTransitionToVerified() throws (e.g., a DB error during the status update), the route returns a 500 even though the vote was already committed. The vote is lost from the client's perspective but exists in the DB — a split-brain error.

OPEN ISSUE CONFIRMED: lib/openrouter.ts getRedditRecommendations() returns [] on any API failure (catch block) and also when the API returns a non-ok response. No error is surfaced to the caller. The scrape route treats an empty array as a definitive 'no results', making network errors and genuine empty results indistinguishable.

OPEN ISSUE CONFIRMED: components/LoadMoreButton.tsx loadMore() has a try/finally but no catch on the fetch or the JSON parse. If fetch() or res.json() throws, the finally block runs (setLoading(false)) but hasMore remains true, offset remains stale, and no user feedback is shown — the button silently re-enables for a retry that may repeat the same failure.

lib/ error strategy is mixed: lib/search.ts throws (Error for RULE_01), lib/openrouter.ts returns null/[] on failure, lib/geo.ts returns null on failure, lib/rules.ts returns boolean. This is a multi-strategy pattern within the same layer.

lib/reddit.ts fetchSubredditPosts() returns [] on any network or parse failure. fetchPostComments() returns [] on failure. scrapeRedditForRestaurants() and fetchCommunityPicksForRestaurant() swallow all errors silently from these helpers — callers receive empty arrays with no signal that a network error occurred.

lib/reddit.ts: validateRedditPost and validateExtractedRestaurant throw ValidationError but are called inside try-catch blocks that swallow the error with a console.warn. The validation contract (throws) is undermined by the caller silently catching and discarding.

app/api/scrape/route.ts has a top-level try-catch that returns 500 with `details: String(error)` — this is an intentional top-level boundary. However, the inner pipeline functions (fetchWithFallback, enrichAndUpsert) do not propagate errors — they catch or swallow internally, making the top-level catch mostly redundant.
lib/geo.ts detectCityFromIp() returns null on any fetch error — appropriate for a fallback-optional operation. extractIp() has no try-catch but cannot throw (pure string operations).
lib/hours.ts isOpenNow() wraps all logic in try-catch returning null — appropriate for a parse utility.

components/RestaurantFeedbackButton.tsx cast() function: on fetch failure or non-ok response, it rolls back the optimistic update and resets voted state. No error message is shown to the user. Silent rollback without feedback.
error strategy_by_directory shows lib/ has 49 throws, 21 returns_null, 14 try_catch — the mix within a single layer is the core concern.

OPEN ISSUE CONFIRMED: triggerScrape in app/api/search/route.ts fires on every request where grouped.length < 3 with no in-flight deduplication, no rate limiting, and no backoff. Multiple rapid searches for the same low-result query will fan out multiple parallel scrape jobs to Reddit and Google Places.

The search route orchestration is clean: parseQuery -> detectCityFromIp fallback -> searchRestaurants -> groupRestaurantsByName -> triggerScrape (conditional). Each step has explicit input/output with no back-and-forth between collaborators.

Data translation for the scrape route happens in enrichAndUpsert — a single well-defined function that maps (ExtractedRestaurant, PlaceDetails) -> DB upsert. This is a clear and bounded translation point.
The scrape state machine (lib/state-machine.ts) provides an explicit protocol contract between scrape pipeline stages. assertValidTransition at each advance point makes the choreography observable.

app/api/scrape/route.ts fetchWithFallback takes a mutable `state` variable and an `advance` callback — the state is owned by POST but mutated via callback inside fetchWithFallback. This is a shared-mutable-state pattern across a boundary; returning the final state from fetchWithFallback would be cleaner.

The geo/route.ts endpoint combines lat/lon coordinate lookup (Nominatim) and IP-based geolocation into one handler, with an internal if/else dispatch. This mixed responsibility at the handler level is workable but the two paths differ in data sources and error modes.

Error propagation across boundaries is partially consistent: most route handlers return NextResponse.json with structured error objects (error, message), but the scrape route's catch block returns `{ error: 'Scrape failed', details: String(error) }` which leaks internal error details to callers.

The /api/trending route directly queries prisma and sorts in-memory — no shared service layer. This is acceptable for a small route but diverges from the /api/search pattern which goes through lib/search.ts.

LoadMoreButton receives `shownNames: string[]` from the server and converts it to a Set client-side. The boundary between server (array) and client (Set) is a minor translation gap that could be made explicit.

The vote routes (app/api/vote/route.ts and app/api/restaurant-vote/route.ts) follow a consistent pattern: validate input -> check rule -> run transaction -> return updated counts. This is clean orchestration.

The two open issues from the previous review cycle remain unresolved, and a new double-parse defect was found in the scrape route.

mid_level_elegance.dimension_character: The codebase has several well-designed seams — the scrape pipeline uses an explicit state machine, enrichment translation is localized to enrichAndUpsert, and the vote routes follow a consistent orchestration pattern. However, two persistent structural defects undermine mid-level elegance: the search-more/LoadMoreButton seam forces grouping logic to be re-implemented client-side due to an asymmetric API contract, and the background scrape trigger has no deduplication guard, creating a fan-out pattern on every low-result search.
mid_level_elegance.score_rationale: A score of 65 reflects clean seams in the vote and scrape enrichment paths offset by two significant boundary design problems that were flagged in a prior review and remain open. The asymmetric grouping contract between /api/search and /api/search-more forces the client to replicate server-side logic, which is a clear mid-level elegance failure. The unconstrained scrape trigger is a correctness risk at the integration boundary.

test_strategy.dimension_character: The test suite has excellent coverage of the two purely mechanical layers — field validation (lib/validate.ts, 57 tests) and state machine transitions (lib/state-machine.ts, 65 tests) — and reasonable coverage of API route request/response contracts. However, the core business logic layer — the restaurant extraction heuristics, the grouping algorithm, the hours parser, the query regex fallback, and the DB rule implementations — is almost entirely untested. The 4 open issues from the prior review remain unresolved.
test_strategy.score_rationale: A score of 62 reflects a test suite that is thorough where it is easy to test (pure validators, state machine, route contracts) but has systematic blind spots at the logic layer that matters most for correctness. The 4 confirmed-open critical path gaps (hours, extractRestaurantName, regexFallback, rules DB logic) each represent functions where bugs would be silent and production-invisible. This is above a failing score because the framework tests exist and are well-structured, but significantly below passing because the core heuristics lack any verification.

api_surface_coherence.dimension_character: The public APIs within lib modules are generally coherent — similar functions use consistent naming prefixes and parameter shapes. The main friction is structural: three modules (geo, search, reddit) mix sync and async exports in their public surfaces, which means callers have to check each function individually rather than relying on a predictable contract. The route-level APIs are mostly consistent, though the search/search-more pair uses different response envelopes.
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: At the 80-range anchor, a developer 'would mostly be well-served but would hit recurring friction'. The sync/async mixing across three modules and the search envelope inconsistency are exactly that kind of recurring friction — not blocking, but noticeable pattern breaks. The vote endpoints and validate functions are strongly consistent. The score lands just below 80 because the mixing pattern spans three separate modules, making it a codebase-level regularity rather than an isolated incident.

authorization_consistency.dimension_character: For an anonymous product with no user accounts, the auth posture is deliberately minimal and consistently applied: all read routes are open, votes use fingerprint deduplication instead of session auth (intentional product decision), and the scrape endpoint has an opt-in token guard. The model is coherent and consistently applied across all routes. The one concrete gap is the geo endpoint returning the client's raw IP address and full Nominatim debug data in its production JSON response.
authorization_consistency.score_rationale: The codebase scores in the low 80s because the auth model is intentionally simple, coherent, and consistently applied — a developer reviewing security can reason about it quickly. The IP leakage in the geo debug field is a real information disclosure defect that pulls the score below 85, but it is isolated and fixable with a single edit. The codebase is not in the 60-70 range because there are no auth inconsistencies between sibling routes and no magic permission strings.

ai_generated_debt.dimension_character: The codebase has been actively cleaned of the main AI debt patterns identified in earlier reviews: debug logging and pass-through wrappers are gone. The remaining code is domain-specific, well-named, and comments explain business rules rather than restating code. The main residual pattern is the mechanically repetitive validator functions in lib/validate.ts — structurally identical null/empty checks across 9 validator functions — which creates visual noise but is at genuine API boundaries.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: At the 84 range, the codebase is solidly clean: no restating comments, no debug logging, no pass-through wrappers, no generic names in domain code. The validation repetition in validate.ts and the STOP_WORDS duplication are the clearest remaining artifacts. These are minor and isolated, keeping the score in the low 80s rather than the 90s where there would be no noticeable noise patterns.

incomplete_migration.dimension_character: The codebase shows no signs of incomplete migration. The TypeScript migration is 100% complete with no JS/TS mixing. Recent migrations (restaurant-grouping extraction, function renaming) have been cleanly finished with all callers updated. There are no deprecated symbols, no migration TODOs, no dual-path confusion, and no compatibility shims. The mechanical scanner found zero migration signals.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: At the 96 range, this dimension is exemplary. There is genuinely nothing to flag — no old+new patterns coexisting, no stale shims, no deprecated-but-called code. The score stops short of 100 only because the codebase is young enough that migration discipline hasn't been tested by a large-scale refactor; what has been migrated (grouping extraction, geo renaming) was done cleanly.

package_organization.dimension_character: The top-level directory layout is idiomatic for a Next.js project and reflects clear ownership boundaries: app/ for routes, components/ for UI, lib/ for logic, prisma/ for data. The coupling matrix shows clean unidirectional flow with no bidirectional edges. The one navigability question is within lib/: 12 files spanning external API integrations, domain rules, and infrastructure utilities coexist flat, which a developer must scan fully to orient to a new concern area.
package_organization.score_rationale: At the 88 range, the organization is strong: no straggler roots, no ambiguous folder names, no coupling direction failures, and the coupling matrix is clean. The lib/ flatness is the only gap — for a 12-file, 1730-LOC directory spanning three distinct concern groups, a developer new to the codebase cannot predict whether geo.ts is an infrastructure utility or a domain rule without opening it. This is real but mild friction, keeping the score below 90 but well above 80.

initialization_coupling.dimension_character: The codebase is largely clean from initialization coupling defects — no network calls or file I/O at module scope, no circular init dependencies, and API key reads are correctly deferred to call time. The main concerns are in lib/reddit.ts, where three environment variables are read at import time and a mutable OAuth token cache is a module-level singleton that crosses request boundaries.
initialization_coupling.score_rationale: The lib/db.ts globalForPrisma pattern is a known Next.js idiom and not a defect. The env-var reads in lib/reddit.ts are a moderate concern for testability but not a runtime bug. The mutable cachedToken is a minor correctness risk in serverless environments. Overall the codebase sits at a middling-good score — most initialization is clean but a few module-level patterns introduce coupling fragility.

design_coherence.dimension_character: The codebase demonstrates solid function-level focus in most lib modules — search, geo, fingerprint, rules, and state-machine are all single-responsibility. The design weaknesses concentrate in the UI layer: TrendingSection is an overloaded component mixing geolocation, fallback orchestration, and rendering; the STATUS badge lookup map is defined independently in three files; and PRICE_ORDER is duplicated between the search page and LoadMoreButton. The scrape route has a redundant double-parse of the query string within a single request lifecycle.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The lib layer is well-structured with clear single responsibilities. The component/route layer shows a pattern of incremental accumulation — constants and status maps repeated across files because there is no shared constants module. The scrape route's double-parse is a concrete inefficiency. These are real but moderate design issues; the codebase is not severely incoherent, just lacking a shared constants home.

contract_coherence.dimension_character: Most of the codebase honors its contracts well — validate functions throw consistently, API route handlers return arrays-or-empty without throwing, and rule-check functions return booleans with clear boolean semantics. The main contract weaknesses are two ParsedQuery interface definitions with differing optionality (one in lib/validate.ts with all fields optional, one in lib/search.ts with terms and raw required), extractIp returning a sentinel string 'unknown' that callers must know about without it being in the type, and the fetchWithFallback function returning a state field that is redundant and potentially confusing.
contract_coherence.score_rationale: The codebase scores above average on contract coherence — no getter-that-mutates patterns, no docstring/signature divergences, and error contracts are consistent within modules. The deductions come from the duplicate ParsedQuery interface with different shapes (a genuine contract inconsistency callers could trip over) and the implicit 'unknown' sentinel from extractIp. These are real but not critical defects.

logic_clarity.dimension_character: The core business logic in lib/ is clear and well-structured — hours parsing, restaurant grouping, state machine transitions, and rule checks all have explicit, distinct branches that match their stated purpose. The weaknesses concentrate in the scrape route's unusual closure-based state mutation pattern and in components where a ref is set but never read. There are no always-true/always-false conditions or truly dead code paths in the critical logic paths.
logic_clarity.score_rationale: The codebase avoids the most common logic clarity failures — no boolean simplification opportunities, no identical branches, no dead code after returns in critical paths. The deductions come from the dead scrapeFinished.current ref, the unused returned state field from fetchWithFallback, and the duplicated direction validation guard. These are real clarity issues but not correctness bugs.

type_safety.dimension_character: The codebase has solid type safety at the lib layer — Prisma types flow correctly through service functions, no ts-ignore suppression, and external API boundary casts are appropriate. The main weaknesses are in the component layer where RestaurantStatus enum values are typed as plain string in interface definitions, causing silent fallback behavior instead of type errors for unexpected statuses, and a structural compatibility cast in lib/search.ts that bypasses TypeScript's structural type checking without explanation.
type_safety.score_rationale: The absence of ts-ignore comments and correct Prisma type usage are meaningful positives. The enum bypass pattern in components (string instead of RestaurantStatus) is a genuine safety gap — components can silently misbehave with unexpected status values. The `as unknown as GroupableRestaurant[]` cast in search.ts is a type lie that hides a real structural compatibility question. These are real but not critical defects in a relatively small codebase.

rule-violations.test.ts imports from lib/validate.ts and lib/search.ts but never imports or calls checkRule01, checkRule03, checkRule05
tryTransitionToVerified has complex logic: fetch restaurant, check status, check RULE_01 (count), update status — none of this is exercised

checkRule01 uses prisma.communityRecommendation.count; checkRule03 uses prisma.vote.findFirst; checkRule05 uses prisma.restaurantVote.findFirst — all mocked at the prisma level in other tests but never in a test that actually calls the rule functions

issues.summary: lib/restaurant-grouping.ts groupRestaurantsByName, shouldMerge, and normalizeWebsite have zero tests despite complex merging logic
issues.suggestion: Add a __tests__/restaurant-grouping.test.ts covering: normalizeWebsite with valid URLs, www-prefixed URLs, invalid URLs; shouldMerge with shared website, prefix-matched names, non-matching pairs; groupRestaurantsByName with duplicates, multi-location chains (same website), excludeNames filtering.
evidence: groupRestaurantsByName uses an O(n^2) iterative merge loop with a `merged` flag — correctness of convergence is unverified
shouldMerge checks website hostname normalization AND name prefix overlap — both are heuristics with edge cases
normalizeWebsite handles URL parse failures with a regex fallback
The function is used in 2 production paths (lib/search.ts and components/LoadMoreButton.tsx) and is the core de-duplication mechanism

issues.summary: lib/geo.ts, lib/search.ts, and lib/reddit.ts mix sync and async exports in their public API surfaces
issues.suggestion: Move sync utility functions that are used outside their home module into a more appropriate home. extractRestaurantName in lib/reddit.ts could move to lib/restaurant-grouping.ts or be unexported if only used internally. groupRestaurantsByName in lib/search.ts is already re-exported from lib/restaurant-grouping.ts — callers should import from there directly, removing it from lib/search.ts's public API. extractIp in lib/geo.ts is legitimately paired with detectCityFromIp since callers always need both; document this pairing in a module comment rather than splitting.
evidence: lib/geo.ts: detectCityFromIp is async, extractIp is sync — both are public exports used together by callers
lib/search.ts: groupRestaurantsByName is sync while parseQuery, searchRestaurants, countSearchResults, findRestaurantGroup are all async

lib/reddit.ts: extractRestaurantName is sync and exported; scrapeRedditForRestaurants and fetchCommunityPicksForRestaurant are async — the sync export has a completely different character from the I/O-bound async exports

issues.summary: GET /api/search and GET /api/search-more return different response envelope shapes for the same result type
issues.suggestion: Make the envelope fields explicit in shared TypeScript types so callers can't mix them up. Alternatively, add a brief comment at the top of each route explaining the intentional envelope difference and why search-more returns ungrouped rows (client-side grouping via excludeNames). This turns an implicit inconsistency into a documented contract.
evidence: /api/search returns {city, terms, results, count} — includes city and terms resolved from the query

/api/search-more returns {results, hasMore} — omits city and terms; the client already has them from the first call so this is intentional, but the result objects themselves are shaped differently (search returns grouped RestaurantGroup[], search-more returns raw RestaurantWithRecommendations[] that the client groups locally)

issues.summary: GET /api/geo returns raw client IP and full Nominatim address data in production JSON response
issues.suggestion: Remove the `debug` field from production responses, or gate it behind a query parameter that is only enabled in development (e.g. `?debug=1` checked against NODE_ENV !== 'production'). The simplest fix: change the response to `return NextResponse.json({ city })` and strip all debug fields. Keep debug information server-side in console.log calls if diagnostics are needed.
evidence: Line 35: `return NextResponse.json({ city, debug: { source: 'ip', ip } })` — the client IP is included in the response body returned to the browser

Line 23: `return NextResponse.json({ city, debug: { source: 'nominatim', status: nominatimStatus, address: data.address } })` — full address object from Nominatim (which may include neighbourhood, postcode, county) is returned to the browser
Line 25: `return NextResponse.json({ city: null, debug: { source: 'nominatim_failed', status: nominatimStatus, body: rawBody.slice(0, 200) } })` — raw upstream error body is returned

issues.summary: lib/validate.ts repeats identical null/empty check pattern across 9 validator functions without abstraction
issues.suggestion: Extend the field-loop pattern used in validateRedditPost to a shared helper: `function requireString(obj: Record<string, unknown>, field: string, context: string): void`. Each validator can then call requireString(obj, 'name', 'Restaurant') instead of repeating the two-branch check inline. This reduces the 274-line file by roughly 40% while keeping each validator's intent clear. The helper stays private within validate.ts.
evidence: Each string field check follows the exact same 4-line pattern: check === null || === undefined, throw; check === '', throw. This pattern is repeated ~25 times across validateParsedQuery, validateRedditPost, validateExtractedRestaurant, validatePlaceDetails, validateRestaurantInput, validateCommunityRecommendationInput, validateRestaurantVoteInput, validateVoteInput.
validateRedditPost uses a field list loop (REDDIT_POST_STRING_FIELDS) for string fields — demonstrating awareness of the pattern — but the other 7 validators duplicate the pattern inline.

issues.summary: STOP_WORDS constant defined independently in lib/search.ts and lib/reddit.ts with overlapping content
issues.suggestion: Rename one or both to reflect their distinct purposes: SEARCH_STOP_WORDS in lib/search.ts and RELEVANCE_STOP_WORDS in lib/reddit.ts. This removes the false impression of shared logic and makes each set's purpose explicit without requiring any extraction or coupling between modules.
evidence: lib/search.ts line 87-91: STOP_WORDS Set with 17 food/query-related words used in parseWords() for DB search filtering
lib/reddit.ts line 162-166: STOP_WORDS Set with 22 words used in isPostRelevantToQuery() for Reddit post relevance checking
The sets overlap but serve different filtering purposes — search.ts filters DB query terms, reddit.ts filters relevance words. The name collision without shared definition is the issue.

issues.summary: lib/ is flat with 12 files spanning three distinct concern groups: external APIs, domain rules, and infrastructure utilities
issues.suggestion: If subdirectory grouping is desired, a staged approach: (1) Create lib/integrations/ and move reddit.ts, google-places.ts, openrouter.ts, geo.ts into it; update import paths in app/api/* and lib/search.ts callers. (2) Optionally create lib/domain/ for search.ts, rules.ts, state-machine.ts, restaurant-grouping.ts. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` after each move to validate imports. Given the project is small (12 files), this is a low-urgency improvement — the flat structure is not blocking today.
evidence: External API integrations: reddit.ts, google-places.ts, openrouter.ts, geo.ts — all fetch from third-party services
Domain rules and logic: search.ts, rules.ts, state-machine.ts, restaurant-grouping.ts — core application behavior
Infrastructure utilities: db.ts, validate.ts, fingerprint.ts, hours.ts — cross-cutting support
All 12 files are at the same lib/ level with no naming prefix or subdirectory to signal grouping. A developer looking for 'where is the Reddit integration?' must scan all 12 names.

issues.summary: Reddit API credentials read at module scope, fragile in testing
issues.suggestion: Move these reads inside `getAccessToken()` or into a lazy getter so that the values are resolved at call time, not import time. This makes the module testable without env var setup before import.
evidence: Line 54: `const USER_AGENT = process.env.REDDIT_USER_AGENT || 'LocalRecos/1.0'`
Line 55: `const CLIENT_ID = process.env.REDDIT_CLIENT_ID`
Line 56: `const CLIENT_SECRET = process.env.REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET`
These are captured once at import time — any test that imports lib/reddit.ts after setting env vars will use stale values if the import was cached.

issues.summary: Mutable OAuth token cache is module-level singleton, leaks across requests in serverless
issues.suggestion: The current caching is acceptable in a long-running server context. For serverless, consider moving token caching to an external store (Redis/KV) or accepting that each cold start re-fetches. At minimum, document that this module assumes single-process execution.
evidence: Line 58: `let cachedToken: { token: string; expiresAt: number } | null = null`
In Next.js serverless (Vercel), each function invocation may reuse the module instance across requests, meaning one user's token refresh can affect another's timing window.
`getAccessToken()` reads and writes this singleton without any lock, creating a potential race condition under concurrent requests.

issues.summary: STATUS badge lookup map defined independently in three files
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared status display map to lib/restaurant-status.ts and import it in all three files. The description field in STATUS_INFO can be an optional property on the shared type.
evidence: TrendingSection.tsx lines 19-23: STATUS_BADGE constant with VERIFIED/UNREVIEWED/INCOMPLETE keys
RestaurantCard.tsx lines 51-55: STATUS_LABELS constant with same keys and shape
app/restaurant/[id]/page.tsx lines 13-29: STATUS_INFO constant with same keys plus description field
All three map the same RestaurantStatus enum values to display labels and Tailwind class names

issues.summary: PRICE_ORDER sort constant duplicated between search page and LoadMoreButton
issues.suggestion: Move PRICE_ORDER to lib/restaurant-grouping.ts (already shared between server and client) and import it in both places.
evidence: app/search/page.tsx line 11: `const PRICE_ORDER: Record<string, number> = { '$': 1, '$$': 2, '$$$': 3, '$$$$': 4 }`
components/LoadMoreButton.tsx line 7: identical definition

issues.summary: Query string parsed twice (two LLM calls) within a single scrape request
issues.suggestion: Return the parsed terms from fetchWithFallback alongside results, or extract parseQuery to the top of POST and pass terms down to fetchWithFallback and the enrichment step.
evidence: fetchWithFallback at line 24 calls `(await parseQuery(query)).terms` — LLM call #1
POST handler at line 194 calls `(await parseQuery(query)).terms` again — LLM call #2
parseQuery delegates to parseQueryWithLLM which makes a real HTTP request to OpenRouter

issues.summary: TrendingSection mixes geolocation strategy, fetch orchestration, and rendering in one component body
issues.suggestion: Extract city detection and trending fetch into a `useTrendingRestaurants()` hook returning `{ city, results, loading }`. The component then only renders based on hook output.
evidence: Single useEffect handles: navigator.geolocation check, success/error callbacks, /api/geo fetch, /api/trending fetch, localStorage write — confirmed >15 cyclomatic branches
Component simultaneously owns city detection strategy, loading state, empty state, and full rendering

issues.reasoning: app/api/scrape/route.ts has zero static importers because Next.js resolves API routes by filesystem path, not import graph. It is a live production endpoint called via HTTP from app/api/search/route.ts (triggerScrape function).
app/api/search-more/route.ts is a legitimate Next.js API route called from LoadMoreButton.tsx via fetch('/api/search-more'). Zero static importers is expected.
app/api/trending/route.ts is called via fetch('/api/trending') from TrendingSection.tsx. Zero static importers is expected for Next.js routes.
app/api/vote/route.ts is a live endpoint called from RecommendationCard component via fetch. Not orphaned.
app/search/page.tsx is a Next.js page component resolved by filesystem routing. Zero static importers is correct.

lib/google-places.ts high cyclomatic complexity reflects the many edge cases in Google Places API response parsing (food type filtering, business status, photo resolution, price mapping). All branches serve a single coherent responsibility: enriching restaurant data from the API.

lib/openrouter.ts has regexFallback which is synchronous, but the exported functions are correctly async because they may call the API. The 'async without await' signal is likely targeting regexFallback itself which is internal and not exported async.

lib/reddit.ts hardcoded URL 'https://www.reddit.com/api/v1/access_token' is Reddit's stable OAuth2 token endpoint. This is a well-known third-party API URL that does not change; no config indirection is needed.
prisma/seed.ts is invoked by `prisma db seed` command configured in package.json. It is a framework seed script, not an orphaned file.

The boilerplate duplication between RestaurantFeedbackButton.tsx and what the signal calls VoteButton.tsx — VoteButton.tsx does not exist as a separate file in components/. RestaurantFeedbackButton and any similar component share an optimistic vote pattern that is intentional and appropriate for the domain.

app/api/geo/route.ts (36 LOC) is a small focused route that handles both GPS coordinate reverse geocoding and IP fallback — two closely related concerns for the same city detection feature. Not truly mixed responsibilities.
app/api/restaurant-vote/route.ts is a live endpoint called from RestaurantFeedbackButton. Not orphaned; the file is accessed via HTTP routing.
app/restaurant/[id]/page.tsx is a Next.js dynamic route page component. Zero static importers is expected.

LoadMoreButton.tsx at 213 LOC contains rendering logic, state management, and group-deduplication logic — these are coherent responsibilities for a stateful pagination component. The PRICE_ORDER duplication is captured separately as a confirmed issue.

issues.summary: ParsedQuery defined twice with different field optionality — lib/validate.ts vs lib/search.ts
issues.suggestion: Remove the private interface from lib/validate.ts and import the canonical ParsedQuery from lib/search.ts (or a shared types file) for the validation function signature. Alternatively, the validate module's interface is intentionally loose to avoid circular imports — in that case, add a comment explaining the divergence.
evidence: lib/validate.ts lines 18-22: `interface ParsedQuery { city?: string | null; terms?: string | null; raw?: string | null; }` — all fields optional
lib/search.ts lines 8-12: `export interface ParsedQuery { city: string | null; terms: string; raw: string; }` — terms and raw are required strings

lib/validate.ts is used by lib/search.ts to validate a ParsedQuery after parseQueryWithLLM returns — the validate-time interface accepts optional fields while the post-validation export promises required fields. This is coherent in practice but a type contract divergence that could confuse consumers importing ParsedQuery from lib/search vs lib/validate.

issues.summary: extractIp returns 'unknown' sentinel string not expressed in return type
issues.suggestion: Either return `string | null` (returning null when no IP is detectable) and update detectCityFromIp to check for null, or document the sentinel in a JSDoc comment. The null approach makes the contract self-documenting via types.
evidence: lib/geo.ts line 42: `export function extractIp(headers: Headers): string` — return type is `string`
lib/geo.ts lines 44-47: returns `headers.get('x-forwarded-for')?.split(',')[0]?.trim() || headers.get('x-real-ip') || 'unknown'`
detectCityFromIp explicitly checks `ip === 'unknown'` as a sentinel — consumers must know this convention
The 'unknown' sentinel is invisible from the function's declared `string` return type

issues.summary: findRestaurantGroup lacks explicit return type annotation on a public API function
issues.suggestion: Add explicit return type `Promise<RestaurantGroup | null>` to make the null-returning contract visible without requiring callers to inspect the implementation.
evidence: lib/search.ts line 68: `export async function findRestaurantGroup(restaurantId: string, city: string, name: string)` — no return type annotation
The function is exported and called from app/restaurant/[id]/page.tsx, making it part of the public API
Return type is inferred as `Promise<RestaurantGroup | null>` from the .find() expression

issues.summary: scrapeFinished.current ref is written but never read — dead state
issues.suggestion: Remove the `scrapeFinished` ref entirely. If it was intended to guard against double-scrape triggers, the `stopped` boolean already serves that role via the cleanup function.
evidence: Line 18: `const scrapeFinished = useRef(false)` — ref is created
Line 37: `scrapeFinished.current = true` — ref is set inside the async run() function
No other line in the file reads scrapeFinished.current — the value is never consumed by rendering or any conditional

issues.summary: fetchWithFallback returns a state field that is always ignored by the caller
issues.suggestion: Remove `state` from the return object of fetchWithFallback since it is never used. The state machine is managed entirely through the `advance` callback closure. Simplify the return type to `{ results: ExtractedRestaurant[]; empty: boolean }`.
evidence: Lines 22: function return type includes `state: ScrapeState` in the destructured object
Line 189: caller destructures `{ results: redditResults, empty }` — `state` is not destructured and is dropped
The returned `state` is the closure variable at the moment of return, which has already been advanced to 'EXTRACTING' on the success path

issues.summary: Direction validation guard duplicated verbatim in two vote route handlers
issues.suggestion: Extract a `parseVoteDirection(direction: string): VoteDirection | null` helper that validates against the VoteDirection enum values and returns null on invalid input. Both routes call this helper and check for null. This also makes the validation consistent with the enum definition.
evidence: app/api/vote/route.ts lines 27-30: `if (direction !== 'up' && direction !== 'down') { return NextResponse.json({ error: 'direction must be "up" or "down"' }, { status: 400 }); }`
app/api/restaurant-vote/route.ts lines 27-30: identical guard with identical error message
The VoteDirection enum from @prisma/client exists (used on line 56 in both files) but is not used for this validation — raw string comparison is used instead

issues.summary: RestaurantStatus enum bypassed with string type in component interfaces
issues.suggestion: Import RestaurantStatus from '@prisma/client' and type the status field as RestaurantStatus in both component interfaces. This makes unexpected status values a type error rather than a silent fallback. The ?? STATUS_LABELS.UNREVIEWED fallback can then be removed as it would be unreachable.
evidence: components/RestaurantCard.tsx line 39: `status: string` in Restaurant interface — RestaurantStatus enum values are VERIFIED, UNREVIEWED, INCOMPLETE
components/TrendingSection.tsx line 14: `status: string` in TrendingRestaurant interface
Both components index their STATUS_BADGE/STATUS_LABELS maps with the status string — an unexpected status value silently falls back to UNREVIEWED instead of being caught at compile time
Prisma's generated RestaurantStatus enum is available in @prisma/client and is correctly used in lib/rules.ts and app/api routes

issues.summary: groupRestaurantsByName wrapper casts through unknown to silence structural type mismatch
issues.suggestion: Make RestaurantWithRecommendations structurally extend GroupableRestaurant, or directly declare `restaurants: GroupableRestaurant[]` as the parameter type for this wrapper. The `as unknown as` double cast is a red flag that should be resolved by proper type alignment.
evidence: lib/search.ts line 61: `return _groupRestaurantsByName(restaurants as unknown as GroupableRestaurant[])`
The input type is RestaurantWithRecommendations[] but _groupRestaurantsByName expects GroupableRestaurant[]
Both interfaces share the same fields — the cast silences TypeScript rather than making the structural compatibility explicit
This hides whether the types are actually compatible at the field level

issues.summary: Vote direction validated as string at runtime but type never narrowed to literal union
issues.suggestion: Type the body as `{ recommendation_id?: string; direction?: 'up' | 'down' }` from the start, or use a type assertion after the guard: `const dir = direction as 'up' | 'down'`. Then the VoteDirection mapping is type-safe and the compiler can verify all cases are covered.
evidence: app/api/vote/route.ts line 11: `let body: { recommendation_id?: string; direction?: string }` — direction is string
Lines 27-30: guard checks direction !== 'up' && direction !== 'down' — this narrows the runtime value but TypeScript still sees direction as `string | undefined` after the guard

Line 56: `const voteDirection = direction === 'up' ? VoteDirection.up : VoteDirection.down` — ternary on an unnarrowed string; if somehow direction is undefined here (defensive guard was satisfied by other means), this would silently produce VoteDirection.down

issues.summary: Null guard on Prisma non-nullable fields suggests type distrust
issues.suggestion: Remove the `?? 0` fallbacks. The Prisma-generated type guarantees upvotes and downvotes are numbers. If defensive coding is desired here, add a comment explaining why.
evidence: Line 65: `const totalNetVotes = (restaurant.upvotes ?? 0) - (restaurant.downvotes ?? 0)`
Prisma schema defines upvotes and downvotes as Int @default(0) — they are non-nullable in the TypeScript types
The ?? 0 fallback is unreachable according to the Prisma type; it suggests the developer was uncertain about the nullability

add.header: Clean one-way dependency graph, no cycles
add.description: All lib/ modules flow upward to app/api/ routes; no circular imports detected. The acyclic structure is consistently maintained across 34 files.

add.header: IP extraction split across two lib modules
add.description: lib/fingerprint.ts and lib/geo.ts both parse x-forwarded-for and x-real-ip independently. This duplication exists because the modules were authored for separate concerns (fingerprinting vs geolocation) without coordinating on the shared header-reading step.

add.header: lib/search.ts acts as a partial facade over restaurant-grouping
add.description: lib/search.ts re-exports types and wraps groupRestaurantsByName from lib/restaurant-grouping.ts, creating an inconsistency since LoadMoreButton.tsx imports from restaurant-grouping directly. The wrapper adds no logic and blurs the boundary.

add.header: Double scrape trigger between server and client
add.description: app/api/search/route.ts fires a server-side scrape when results < 3, while SearchPoller.tsx unconditionally fires a client-side scrape on every search mount. Both call the same /api/scrape endpoint, creating duplicate concurrent pipeline runs per search.

add.header: lib/ file naming clearly maps to domain concerns
add.description: Each lib/ file name communicates its purpose (geo, rules, hours, reddit, state-machine). A new engineer can navigate the logic layer without needing documentation.

add.header: Consistent named exports in lib, default exports in components
add.description: lib/ uses exclusively named exports; components/ uses exclusively default exports. This division is consistent across all 22 files in those two directories and matches TypeScript/React conventions.

add.header: PRICE_ORDER constant duplicated in two consumer files
add.description: Both components/LoadMoreButton.tsx and app/search/page.tsx define identical PRICE_ORDER maps locally rather than sharing from a single source. Arose from independent local definitions without a subsequent consolidation pass.

add.header: Sibling vote routes follow identical structural protocol
add.description: app/api/vote/route.ts and app/api/restaurant-vote/route.ts mirror each other in structure (body parse, fingerprint, duplicate check, transaction, state transition) — a positive behavioral consistency signal.

add.header: lib/ layer uses mixed error strategies without rationale
add.description: lib/search.ts throws, lib/openrouter.ts returns [], lib/geo.ts returns null, lib/rules.ts returns boolean — all within the same layer. The variety is not documented and creates unpredictable caller behavior.

add.header: I/O boundary helpers silently return empty on failure
add.description: Reddit fetching, OpenRouter calls, and comment fetching all return [] on any error. This makes callers unable to distinguish network failures from genuine empty results, which affects pipeline state transitions.

add.header: Top-level route catch blocks provide consistent 500 boundary
add.description: All API routes wrap their main logic in try-catch returning { error, details } with status 500 — a consistent top-level boundary pattern. The weakness is that inner helpers swallow errors before reaching this boundary.

add.header: Validate functions follow consistent validateXxx pattern
add.description: All 8 validator functions in lib/validate.ts use the validateXxx naming convention, making them easy to discover and predict. A developer adding a new entity type knows exactly what to name the validator.

add.header: Reddit helper predicates accurately named with isX pattern
add.description: isRecommendationRequest, isPostRelevantToQuery, isValidRestaurantName in lib/reddit.ts all return boolean and have names that accurately describe their predicates. No name/behavior mismatch.

add.header: Rule functions use opaque code names not business intent
add.description: checkRule01/03/05 in lib/rules.ts encode manifest rule codes rather than intent. Callers must read the implementation or comments to understand what is being checked. This is a developer experience cost on every read path.

add.header: Scrape pipeline helpers are genuine abstractions
add.description: fetchWithFallback and enrichAndUpsert in the scrape route each encapsulate real multi-step behavior with state machine coordination and I/O — they add value proportional to their indirection.

add.header: Validate.ts parallel interfaces driven by circular import constraint
add.description: The local interface definitions in lib/validate.ts mirror types in lib/reddit.ts and lib/search.ts to break circular imports. This is a structural workaround, not an abstraction choice, and is documented in the file header.

add.header: Module-level functions avoid class ceremony throughout
add.description: No delegation-heavy classes exist. All abstractions are module-level functions, keeping the codebase flat and easy to trace.

add.header: Minimal production dependency footprint by design
add.description: Only four production deps: Next.js, React, react-dom, @prisma/client. All network calls use native fetch, all validation is hand-rolled, no utility or date libraries. This keeps the bundle small but shifts maintenance burden to custom code in lib/validate.ts and lib/hours.ts.

add.header: prisma/client correctly placed in dependencies
add.description: After a previous fix, @prisma/client is in production dependencies and prisma CLI is in devDependencies. The placement is now correct for deployment scenarios where devDeps are pruned.

add.header: Scrape route POST handler partially decomposed
add.description: fetchWithFallback and enrichAndUpsert extract the two major sub-concerns from the scrape POST handler. The handler itself is now orchestration-level but still coordinates state advances, validation calls, and calls to parseQuery twice for the same input.

add.header: Pure utility functions have clean, intention-revealing control flow
add.description: lib/hours.ts, lib/restaurant-grouping.ts, and the extraction logic in lib/reddit.ts use well-named helpers, clear dispatch patterns, and appropriate levels of extraction. These are positive examples of low-level elegance.

add.header: Vote routes follow consistent orchestration pattern
add.description: Both /api/vote and /api/restaurant-vote use the same validate -> check-rule -> transaction -> return-counts pattern. The seam between the route and the rules/db layer is clean and predictable.

add.header: Scrape enrichment translation localized to enrichAndUpsert
add.description: The translation from (ExtractedRestaurant, PlaceDetails) to DB write happens in a single function with clear inputs and outputs. No ad-hoc mapping scattered across the handler.

add.header: Asymmetric grouping contract between search and search-more
add.description: The initial /api/search endpoint returns grouped RestaurantGroup objects while /api/search-more returns raw RestaurantWithRecommendations arrays. This forces the client to perform server-equivalent grouping and maintain deduplication state.

add.header: Validation and state machine layers are thoroughly tested
add.description: lib/validate.ts has 57 adversarial field tests and lib/state-machine.ts has 65 transition tests. These mechanical layers are well-covered and the tests are structured and intention-revealing.

add.header: Route contract tests exist but mock all lib dependencies
add.description: All 5 API route test files verify request/response contracts but mock lib/db, lib/search, lib/rules, lib/geo, and lib/openrouter. Implementation logic in those modules has no coverage from the route test layer.

add.header: No snapshot tests — all behavioral assertions
add.description: The test suite uses only behavioral assertions (expect(value).toBe/toEqual/toMatch). No snapshot overuse exists, which is a positive signal for test maintenance.

add.header: Core extraction heuristics entirely untested
add.description: The restaurant name extraction (extractRestaurantName, isValidRestaurantName), hours parsing (isOpenNow), query fallback (regexFallback), and grouping algorithm (groupRestaurantsByName) have zero test coverage. These are the highest-value functions to test because bugs are silent in production.

add.header: Vote endpoints use consistent response envelope
add.description: Both /api/vote and /api/restaurant-vote return {success, upvotes, downvotes, net_votes} with identical structure, and both validate direction as 'up'|'down' before acting. This makes them predictable to call in tandem.

add.header: No public functions with excessive parameter counts
add.description: All public lib functions have 4 or fewer parameters; the widest is searchRestaurants(city, terms, limit, offset). No cases of parameter-object smell in public APIs.

add.header: Sync utilities mixed into async module surfaces
add.description: Three lib modules export both sync and async functions at the same level, requiring callers to check each export's async character rather than relying on a uniform contract.

add.header: Intentionally anonymous product, no session auth
add.description: All routes are public by design — there are no user accounts. Vote deduplication uses fingerprinting (RULE_03/RULE_05) rather than session auth, a documented product decision that trades off strict identity for anonymous participation.

add.header: Consistent input validation across all write endpoints
add.description: All POST routes validate body structure and required fields before acting, returning 400 for malformed input. This is a uniform trust-boundary pattern applied consistently.

add.header: Opt-in internal token guard on expensive scrape endpoint
add.description: POST /api/scrape checks x-internal-token against INTERNAL_API_TOKEN env var — when set, it rejects unauthenticated requests (403). The opt-in design allows local dev without config while enabling production gating.

add.header: Comments explain business rules, not code
add.description: Throughout the codebase, inline comments reference RULE_01/RULE_03/RULE_04/RULE_05/RULE_06 constraints, explain caching decisions, and describe non-obvious fallback logic. No restating comments found.

add.header: Domain-specific naming with no generic handler names
add.description: Functions throughout the scrape, search, and vote pipeline use domain vocabulary: fetchWithFallback, enrichAndUpsert, upsertRestaurant, tryTransitionToVerified, computeFingerprint. No handleData or processItem patterns.

add.header: Validator repetition at DB write boundaries
add.description: lib/validate.ts has 9 structurally identical validator functions. The repetition is at genuine API boundaries (pre-DB-write guards) so it passes the skip rule, but the mechanical nature creates visual noise that a loop pattern could eliminate.

add.header: Uniform TypeScript, no JS/TS file mixing
add.description: All production source files are TypeScript. Only framework config files (next.config.js) remain as JS, which is framework-imposed. The TypeScript migration is fully complete.

add.header: Completed migrations are cleanly finished
add.description: The restaurant-grouping extraction and the detect_city rename were both completed with all callers updated — no partial migrations or stale aliases remain. This demonstrates clean migration discipline.

add.header: Uniform HTTP client — fetch used throughout
add.description: All HTTP calls use the native fetch API. No axios, node-fetch, or mixed HTTP client patterns across the codebase.

add.header: Clean unidirectional coupling across directories
add.description: The coupling matrix shows only app/→components/ and components/→lib/ edges, with no bidirectional or circular directory dependencies. This is the ideal flow for a Next.js project.

add.header: Framework-idiomatic top-level layout
add.description: The top-level directories (app/, components/, lib/, prisma/) map directly to Next.js conventions. A developer familiar with the framework can orient immediately without reading documentation.

add.header: lib/ flat with three implicit concern groups
add.description: lib/ contains 12 files spanning external API integrations (reddit, google-places, openrouter, geo), domain rules (search, rules, state-machine, restaurant-grouping), and infrastructure (db, validate, fingerprint, hours). These concern groups are not reflected in the directory structure.

add.header: PrismaClient globalForPrisma is framework idiom
add.description: The lib/db.ts singleton pattern using globalThis is the official Next.js + Prisma recommended approach to avoid multiple client instances during hot reload. It is not an initialization coupling defect.

add.header: API keys deferred to call time except in reddit.ts
add.description: lib/openrouter.ts and lib/google-places.ts correctly read OPENROUTER_API_KEY and GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY inside the function body at call time. lib/reddit.ts is the outlier that reads credentials at module scope.

add.header: Next.js routes appear orphaned in static import analysis
add.description: All app/api/*/route.ts and app/**/page.tsx files have zero static importers because Next.js resolves them by filesystem path at runtime, not via import graph. The orphaned signal is a systematic false positive for this project's architecture.

add.header: No shared constants module leads to display constant duplication
add.description: The STATUS badge/label maps and PRICE_ORDER are duplicated across 3 and 2 files respectively because there is no shared lib/constants.ts. This is a recurrent pattern that will grow as more components need these values.

add.header: lib layer has clean single-responsibility functions
add.description: lib/rules.ts, lib/state-machine.ts, lib/geo.ts, lib/fingerprint.ts, and lib/hours.ts all have single coherent responsibilities with no mixed concerns. The design discipline is stronger in the lib layer than in components.

add.header: Validate module uses local interface mirrors intentionally
add.description: lib/validate.ts defines local interface copies (ParsedQuery, RedditPost, etc.) with all-optional fields to avoid circular imports from lib/search.ts and lib/reddit.ts. The comment in the file explains this design choice. The loose optionality is intentional for the validation layer.

add.header: Error contracts are consistent within each module
add.description: All validate* functions throw ValidationError, all API route handlers return JSON responses without rethrowing, and all lib functions that may fail return null/empty-array rather than throwing. Error strategy is consistent within each layer.

add.header: Hours parsing complexity is inherent to format variation
add.description: lib/hours.ts handles three distinct hours string formats from Google Places (12h, 24h, compact). The branching complexity is necessary and correct — not a logic clarity defect.

add.header: Scrape route uses closure mutation for state machine
add.description: app/api/scrape/route.ts passes both a `state` string and an `advance` callback to fetchWithFallback. The advance function mutates the outer let variable via closure. This non-standard pattern causes the returned state field to be stale/unused and adds conceptual overhead without benefit.

add.header: Components define local interfaces to avoid server-only imports
add.description: Next.js client components cannot import from server-only modules. Components define their own Restaurant/Recommendation interfaces that mirror Prisma types — this is why RestaurantStatus enum is not used in component interfaces. The workaround is valid but loses enum safety.

add.header: No ts-ignore suppression anywhere in the codebase
add.description: A search of all TypeScript files confirms zero @ts-ignore or @ts-expect-error comments. Type errors are either resolved properly or (in the one case) worked around with an explicit unknown cast.

add.header: External API boundaries use appropriate Record<string, unknown> casts
add.description: lib/google-places.ts and lib/openrouter.ts correctly type external JSON responses as Record<string, unknown> and cast individual fields inline. This is the appropriate TypeScript pattern for untyped external APIs.
